mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving near-term corporate earnings revisions upward and pushing the S&P 500 higher within one week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the Spearman correlation between UNRATE changes and 5-day returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding residential mortgage debt indicate synchronized credit and construction demand expansion. This joint signal predicts stronger aggregate demand and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST and RSAFS moving in the same direction) shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, or the joint signal generates losses in over 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, sustained industrial production growth exceeds capacity utilization, triggering supply-side bottlenecks and input cost inflation that compress equity margins and depress S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates positively with 60-day forward returns during periods where capacity utilization exceeds 80%, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly real GDP growth momentum directly translates to higher corporate revenue expectations. This fundamental growth signal drives immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as analysts update earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between GDPC1 revisions and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.05 for three consecutive years, or the signal produces negative returns in the majority of positive growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly federal funds rate increase above 0.25% signals active monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and reduces present valuations for long-duration assets, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances following a rate hike above the threshold, or the magnitude of rate increases shows no inverse relationship with subsequent equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth exceeding 200,000 and an unemployment rate decline indicate a self-reinforcing labor market recovery. This dual improvement sustains consumer demand and equity earnings growth over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when PAYEMS and UNRATE move in opposite directions, or the interaction effect disappears after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary growth environment, rising real GDP combined with stable or falling consumer prices expands profit margins without triggering aggressive central bank tightening, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns when CPI growth exceeds 2.5% annually, or the combination of GDP growth and low inflation fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 3-year rolling window.
